tool.name: Muninn verify_patch
tool.summary: Semi-formal patch verification. Python module; sends a diff plus context to Claude and parses a structured VERDICT/CONFIDENCE/SUMMARY footer. Optional tracking writes to a Turso DB for post-merge calibration via review/stamp actions.
tool.description: Wraps a fixed semi-formal-reasoning prompt around a model invocation. Inputs are a unified diff (or before/after pair), optional surrounding code as context, and a description of what the patch should do. The model is asked to identify which function definitions are actually invoked, trace before/after execution, check regressions on touched paths, and emit a three-line VERDICT/CONFIDENCE/SUMMARY footer that the tool parses. Three actions: verify (the analysis), review (read past tracking memories for calibration), stamp (record the actual post-merge outcome of a prior verify call). Written as a consumer test of install-manifest-spec v0.3 — the second after muninn-bsky-card. Stresses: tool-to-tool dependency on a memory store, conditional-credential semantics (TURSO_* required only when track=true), variable-input cost, smoke tests that themselves cost money, and stateless tools whose only kill switch is 'rotate the API key out of band'. Findings filed in the muninns-inbox discussion thread linked below.

author.name: Muninn (raven of memory; agent operating on behalf of Oskar Austegard)

env.name: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
env.prompt: Anthropic API key (sk-ant-...). The tool issues one chat-completion call per verify action against api.anthropic.com (or a Cloudflare AI Gateway proxy if CF_* are set). The key is a coarse credential — anything you grant here can use any model on the account, not just the one this tool defaults to. Treat as a password.

env.name: CF_ACCOUNT_ID
env.prompt: Optional Cloudflare account ID for routing Anthropic API calls through a Cloudflare AI Gateway (for caching, observability, or rate-limit pooling). Leave blank to call api.anthropic.com directly. If set, CF_GATEWAY_ID and CF_API_TOKEN must also be set.

env.name: CF_GATEWAY_ID
env.prompt: Optional Cloudflare AI Gateway ID, paired with CF_ACCOUNT_ID. Free-form slug you chose when creating the gateway in the Cloudflare dashboard.

env.name: CF_API_TOKEN
env.prompt: Optional Cloudflare API token scoped to AI Gateway access. Required if CF_ACCOUNT_ID and CF_GATEWAY_ID are set. Treat as a secret.

env.name: TURSO_TOKEN
env.prompt: Conditional: Turso database auth token used by the tracking layer. Required ONLY if you call verify with track=true (the default) or use the review/stamp actions; leave blank for stateless verify-only usage. The spec has no native way to express 'required given an input flag', so this is declared optional and the tool surfaces a runtime error if you set track=true without it. The token grants read+write on the tracking database.

env.name: TURSO_URL
env.prompt: Conditional: hostname of the Turso libSQL database (e.g. 'mydb-username.turso.io'). Same conditional rule as TURSO_TOKEN. Tracking records are written via the libSQL HTTP pipeline endpoint.

scopes.rationale: Each verify call sends the supplied patch and context to Anthropic's chat-completion API and consumes tokens against the user's account. Cost is variable in the size of the patch and context; the user is paying per call.
Tool talks to api.anthropic.com (or gateway.ai.cloudflare.com if CF_* are set) for inference. Optionally talks to the configured Turso libSQL host for tracking writes. No other outbound destinations.

When track=true (default), verify writes a 'pending-review' decision memory to the configured Turso DB so that the review/stamp actions can later compute calibration stats. The DB is the user's own infrastructure, not a third party.

actions.name: verify
actions.summary: Run semi-formal verification on a code patch and return a structured verdict.
actions.description: Synchronously calls the configured Claude model with a fixed prompt template that asks for premise extraction, function-resolution tracing, before/after execution, regression check on touched paths, and edge cases. Parses the model's three-line footer (VERDICT/CONFIDENCE/SUMMARY). Default model is claude-sonnet-4-6 at temperature 0; max_tokens 3000. Cost is variable in patch+context size; budget ~$0.003 per call at the default model for a small diff. SECONDARY EFFECT: when track=true (default), writes a memory row to the Turso DB tagged 'verify-patch-tracking' / 'pending-review'. The spec models actions[].side_effects as a single enum so this secondary effect is not visible in the catalog — flagged in findings.

docs.goal: Get a structured verdict on whether a code diff fixes what it claims without breaking what it preserves.
docs.inputs_brief: patch (req), context, description, model (default claude-sonnet-4-6), repo, pr_num, track (default true)
docs.outputs_brief: {verdict: CORRECT|LIKELY_CORRECT|CONCERNS|BUGGY|PARSE_FAIL, confidence, summary, analysis, elapsed_s, model, tracking_id}

input.patch:
- for i in range(n):
+ for i in range(n + 1):
input.description: Loop was missing the final element

output.summary: Range now covers indices 0..n inclusive; verify call sites expect that semantic.

actions.name: review
actions.summary: Compute calibration stats from past verify tracking memories.
actions.description: Queries the Turso DB for memories tagged 'verify-patch-tracking', tallies them by outcome (caught_bug / useful / correct_pass / false_alarm / waste / pending), and returns the stats plus the list of pending records that have not yet been stamped. Read-only; cannot run without TURSO_TOKEN+TURSO_URL.

docs.goal: Look at how well past verify calls predicted real outcomes.
docs.inputs_brief: n (int, default 20, max records to scan)
docs.outputs_brief: {stats: {total, pending, useful, waste, caught_bug, false_alarm, correct_pass}, pending: [tracking_record...]}

actions.name: stamp
actions.summary: Record the actual outcome of a past verify call (post-merge ground truth).
actions.description: Updates a tracking memory in place — supersedes the prior 'pending-review' record with the same body but OUTCOME: <enum> in the summary line, and adjusts confidence per a fixed map (CAUGHT_BUG=0.95, USEFUL=0.85, CORRECT_PASS=0.7, FALSE_ALARM=0.3, WASTE=0.2). Use after the patch has actually merged and you know whether the verify call's verdict matched reality.

docs.goal: Close the calibration loop: turn 'pending' verify tracking records into ground-truth outcomes.
docs.inputs_brief: tracking_id (string from a prior verify call), outcome (enum: CAUGHT_BUG|USEFUL|CORRECT_PASS|FALSE_ALARM|WASTE), note (string, optional)
docs.example: stamp tracking_id=abc123 outcome=CAUGHT_BUG note='caught the off-by-one before merge'

transmits.purpose: model-inference (proxied through Cloudflare AI Gateway when CF_* are set; identical payload as direct-to-Anthropic but Cloudflare also sees it)
